description: Original tasks before rewriting based on actual data

tasks.name: Bookmark the most recent PC patch notes released in the last 14 days
tasks.description: Find the latest PC patch notes article from the last 14 days and add it to your reading list so that exactly one qualifying article is saved.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage by clicking the site logo or entering the main URL.
In the main navigation bar, click the "Patch Notes" section.
On the Patch Notes page, open the platform filter dropdown and select "PC".
Locate the date range filter and choose the preset option labeled "Last 14 days".
Click the sort dropdown and select the option "Newest First".
From the filtered results, click the title of the first patch notes article in the list.
On the patch notes article page, click the "Add to Reading List" or bookmark icon to save the article.
In the top navigation, click the "Reading List" link and then click the saved patch notes title to open it again.

tasks.name: Subscribe to weekly email updates for Patch Notes and Esports
tasks.description: Configure newsletter preferences so that the email address player@example.com receives weekly emails only about Patch Notes and Esports updates.
steps: From the homepage, scroll to the header or footer and click the link labeled "Newsletter" or "Email Updates".
On the newsletter preferences page, click into the email address input field and type "player@example.com".
Open the frequency dropdown menu and select "Weekly" (not "Daily" or "Monthly").
In the topics or categories section, check the box for "Patch Notes".
Check the box for "Esports" and uncheck any other topics that might already be selected, such as "Promotions" or "Community Stories".
If a region dropdown is available, open it and select "North America" as the region.
Click the "Save Preferences" or "Subscribe" button to submit the form.
After the preferences page confirms submission, click the site logo in the header to navigate back to the homepage.

tasks.name: Save the top 3 most-viewed 2025 esports tournament announcements to favorites
tasks.description: Filter esports news to 2025 tournament announcements, sort by most viewed, and add the top 3 tournament articles to your favorites list.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Esports" or "Tournaments" link in the main navigation.
On the Esports news page, locate the date filter and set the start date to "2025-01-01" and the end date to "2025-12-31".
In the category or tag filter area, select the option labeled "Tournament Announcement" or check the tag "Tournament".
Open the sort dropdown and select "Most Viewed" or "Most Popular".
From the sorted results, click the title of the first article in the list that includes the word "Tournament".
On that article page, click the "Add to Favorites" or heart icon to mark it as a favorite.
Use the site breadcrumb or the browser back button to return to the filtered Esports tournament results list.
Click the second and then the third "Tournament" articles in the list one by one, and on each article page click the "Add to Favorites" or heart icon.
In the header, click your profile icon or username and select "Favorites" to open the list of favored articles.

tasks.name: Comment on the balance patch article with the higher version number
tasks.description: Search for balance patch news, compare the first two results by their version numbers in the titles, and post a comment on the article with the higher version number.
steps: On the homepage, click the search icon or search bar in the header.
Type "balance patch" into the search field and press Enter to run the search.
On the search results page, open the sort dropdown and select "Newest First".
From the results, click the title of the first article that includes a version number (for example, "Update 1.3.5") to open it.
Use the browser back button or the search breadcrumb to return to the balance patch search results list.
Click the title of the second article in the results list that also contains a version number in its title to open it.
After viewing the second article's title, use the breadcrumb or back button to return again to the same search results list.
Compare the version numbers in the titles of the first and second results, then click the title of whichever article has the higher version number.
On the chosen article page, scroll to the comments section, type "Excited to try this higher-version balance patch!" into the comment text area, and click the "Post Comment" button.

tasks.name: Configure notifications to only receive server status and maintenance alerts
tasks.description: Adjust account notification settings so that only Server Status and Maintenance notifications are enabled, with in-site notifications on and mobile push turned off.
steps: From any page on the site, click your profile avatar or username in the top navigation bar.
In the dropdown menu that appears, click the "Notification Settings" option.
On the Notification Settings page, locate the list of notification types and toggle ON the switch for "Server Status" alerts.
Toggle ON the switch for "Maintenance" announcements.
Toggle OFF the switches for other categories such as "Promotional Offers", "Esports News", and any additional categories besides "Server Status" and "Maintenance".
In the delivery methods section, uncheck the "Mobile Push" option and ensure that only "In-site Notifications" is checked, leaving email unchecked if it is present.
Set a quiet hours period by clicking the "Do Not Disturb" start time field, selecting "22:00", then clicking the end time field and selecting "08:00".
Scroll to the bottom of the page and click the "Save Changes" button to apply your notification preferences.

tasks.name: Create a reading list of 3 roadmap dev diaries from the last 6 months
tasks.description: Filter the developer blog to roadmap posts from the last 6 months, then create a 'Roadmap Reading' collection containing the first 3 articles in oldest-first order.
steps: On the homepage, click the "Developer Blog" link in the main navigation.
On the Developer Blog page, in the tag or topic filter, select the tag labeled "Roadmap".
In the date filter area, choose the preset option labeled "Last 6 months".
Open the sort dropdown and select "Oldest First" so that the earliest roadmap posts in the last 6 months appear at the top.
From the filtered list, click the first roadmap article title.
On that article page, click the "Add to Collection" button and choose the option to "Create New Collection".
In the collection name input field, type "Roadmap Reading" and click the "Create" or "Create Collection" button.

Use the "Back to Blog" link or the browser back button to return to the Roadmap-filtered list, then open the second and third articles in the list one by one and add each to the existing "Roadmap Reading" collection using the "Add to Collection" button.
In the top navigation, click "Collections" or "My Lists", then select the "Roadmap Reading" collection to view the 3 added dev diary articles.

tasks.name: Bookmark the earliest European announcement of the 'Shadow Siege' update
tasks.description: Search for 'Shadow Siege' update news, filter to Europe region and update announcements, sort by oldest first, and bookmark the earliest European announcement.
steps: From the homepage, click the search bar in the header.
Type "Shadow Siege" into the search field and press Enter to display results.
On the search results page, open the region filter dropdown and select "Europe".
In the category filter section, select the option labeled "Update Announcement" or similar.
Open the sort dropdown and select "Oldest First" to show the earliest announcements first.
From the filtered results, click the title of the first article in the list, which is the earliest European "Shadow Siege" announcement.
On the announcement article page, click the bookmark or "Save" icon to add it to your favorites or reading list.
In the header, click the "Favorites" or "Reading List" link and then click the saved "Shadow Siege" announcement title to open it again.
Use the site logo in the header to return to the homepage after opening the bookmarked announcement.

tasks.name: Add two beginner guides with 10+ minute read time to a 'New Players' collection
tasks.description: Filter game guides to beginner content with a minimum 10-minute reading time, then create a 'New Players' collection containing the top two highest-rated guides.
steps: On the homepage, click the "Guides" or "How to Play" section in the main navigation.
On the Guides page, open the audience filter dropdown and select "Beginner" or "New Players".
Locate the reading time filter or slider and set the minimum reading time to "10 minutes" and the maximum to "30 minutes".
Open the sort dropdown and choose "Highest Rated" or "Most Helpful".
From the filtered and sorted list, click the first guide in the list to open its detail page.
On the guide page, click the "Add to Collection" button and choose the option to "Create New Collection".
In the collection name field, type "New Players" and click the "Create & Add" or similar button to create the collection and add the guide.

Return to the filtered guides list using the "Back to Guides" link or browser back button, then click the second guide in the list and add it to the existing "New Players" collection using the "Add to Collection" control.
In the top navigation, click "Collections" or "My Lists", open the "New Players" collection, and then click the first guide title inside that collection.
